_description: The universal Bug Ticket intake schema. Every department and the command center itself uses this schema when filing a bug to the ZHC Bugs Department. A department that hits a bug files this ticket FIRST, then continues stabilizing per its own SOPs. Filing is mandatory: an unfiled bug is a future repeat.
_usage: Submit a JSON object matching this schema to the Bug Intake Clerk. All fields marked required must be present and non-null. Optional fields default to null.

bug_id._note: Leave null when submitting; the Intake Clerk assigns this.

reported_at._note: Leave null when submitting; the Intake Clerk stamps this.

symptom._description: Verbatim error text or a precise, specific description of the failure. Vague descriptions like 'it broke' are not valid. The Intake Clerk will return malformed tickets for correction.

evidence_paths._description: Paths to checkpoints, logs, QC reports, screenshots, or any other evidence files that document the failure.

severity_guess._description: The reporter's best estimate of severity. The Triage Analyst will confirm or override this. P0 = live run halted or credits burning; P1 = running but producing incorrect output; P2 = edge case or formatting issue, no immediate client impact; P3 = proactive improvement suggestion.

suspected_layer._description: The reporter's best guess at which layer contains the defect. code = a script or binary; sop = an instruction in a role SOP; core-file = AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, MEMORY.md, or bootstrap files; settings-json = openclaw.json or other configuration; model = AI model behavior; external-api = a third-party API contract changed or failed; environment = keys, disk, RAM, network; gap = no SOP covers this situation; unknown = genuinely unclear.

client_slug._description: The slug identifying the client whose box or workflow was affected.

status._note: Set to REPORTED by the Intake Clerk. All subsequent transitions are owned by the Bugs Department and Healer per SOP B-9.3.

dedup_of._description: If this ticket is a duplicate of a prior ticket, the Triage Analyst sets this to the original bug_id.

assigned_healer._description: The slug of the Healer role assigned to this ticket by the Triage Analyst.

kanban_card_id._description: The id of the Kanban card opened for this ticket.
